title: 2. Architectural Design
content:
2. Architectural Design
• Layered Modules: Hierarchical yet interdependent structure: Phonology (sound patterns) → Morphology (word formation) → Syntax (structure) → Semantics (meaning) → Pragmatics (contextual use) → Discourse (narrative flow) → Sociolinguistics (social variation) → Iconographics (visual symbols) → Computational (AI orchestration).
• Data Representations: Use graph-based schemas (e.g., directed acyclic graphs for syntactic trees) and tensor embeddings for multimodal data (e.g., vector spaces unifying text, emoji, and audio waveforms).
• Pipelines and Flows: Bidirectional pipelines allow bottom-up analysis (e.g., phoneme extraction to discourse synthesis) and top-down generation (e.g., pragmatic intent to phonological output). Cross-layer flows employ transformers for multilingual alignment.
3. Semiotic and Multimodal Integration
• Symbol Coexistence: A unified semiotic algebra translates modalities (e.g., emoji as pragmatic enhancers mapped to semantic equivalents in hieroglyphics).
• Translation Mechanisms: Category theory functors map between symbol systems, ensuring bidirectionality (e.g., right-to-left Arabic text integrates with left-to-right emoji sequences).
context: From document: Theorizing the Design and Representation of a Rhetorical-Linguistic… — chunk 3 (chunk 3/4)
